title: Inventory ISO/IWA standards, laboratory test data, field-performance studies, and compliance-verification systems for clean cookstoves
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates ISO/IWA standards documentation, laboratory test data, field-performance studies, and compliance-verification system assessments into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit lab-field gap and verification-coverage documentation.
inputs: ISO 19867-1 and IWA clean-cooking standards documentation
Global Alliance for Clean Cookstoves monitoring documentation
WHO household air pollution health-burden data
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly lab-field gap documentation and verification-system coverage.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering lab-field gap documentation, verification-coverage rates, and stove-category specificity.

title: Analyze laboratory-to-field performance gaps for major stove categories in at least two LMIC contexts
outcome: A laboratory-to-field performance gap analysis for at least two stove categories (improved biomass, LPG, ethanol, biogas) in at least two LMIC contexts, documenting measurement methodology, degradation factors, and stove-category-specific results.
inputs: Published lab-field comparison studies for target stove categories
ISO/IWA laboratory test protocols for reference
Fuel-moisture and cooking-practice data for degradation-factor analysis
expected_artifact: validation.md update with lab-field gap methodology and stove-category results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms lab-field comparison methodology, measurement-method documentation, and stove-category-specific degradation estimates for each target context.
done_condition: Lab-field gap analysis includes methodology, stove-category results, degradation-factor documentation, and replication status for at least two contexts.

title: Assess compliance-verification system coverage across LMIC countries
outcome: A compliance-verification system assessment documenting which countries have independent testing infrastructure, verification-coverage rates across deployed stoves, and the gap between laboratory certification and field verification.
inputs: National testing-laboratory documentation for target countries
Global Alliance for Clean Cookstoves monitoring data
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with verification methodology and coverage-rate documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms verification-system assessment methodology, coverage-rate documentation, and lab-vs-field verification distinction for each target country.
done_condition: Verification assessment includes methodology, coverage rates, lab-vs-field verification distinction, and replication status.

title: Red-team laboratory-performance overstatement and health-impact extrapolation risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how laboratory performance data could overstate field emission reductions, how health-impact claims based on lab data could be misleading, and how compliance claims could create false confidence in stove programs.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every performance claim has lab-vs-field distinction, every health-impact claim has field-performance basis or explicit lab-data caveat, and every compliance claim has verification-coverage documentation. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every performance and health-impact claim is either blocked, softened to lab-data-limited language with explicit field-verification caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match clean-cooking program and manufacturer decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the lab-field gap and compliance-verification analysis would change any real decision for clean-cooking programs, stove manufacturers, or health ministries without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for clean-cooking and manufacturer audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that lab-performance data is used for health-impact claims without field verification, or that compliance claims create false confidence in stove programs. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Kenya Clean Cooking Association program director), named decision (e.g., stove procurement standards), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for health-impact claims based on lab data alone), and replicated field-reality review.
